articles.author: Lorian Belanger
articles.description: Across Iran’s capital, explosions rang out overnight as the U.S. and Israel pounded Iran with airstrikes. Iran and its allies have hit back against Israel and neighboring Gulf states.
articles.title: Live updates: Explosions heard in Tehran and Jerusalem on fifth day of war

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Vanessa Gera, Cara Anna, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Drew Callister, Lisa Marie Pane
articles.description: A U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship Wednesday, as the U.S. and Israel intensify their bombardment of Iran’s security forces.
articles.title: Live updates: Senate rejects war powers bill to halt attacks against Iran

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Vanessa Gera, Cara Anna, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Drew Callister, Lisa Marie Pane
articles.description: Senate Republicans voted down an effort Wednesday to halt President Donald Trump’s war against Iran.
articles.title: Live updates: Senate rejects war powers bill to halt attacks against Iran

articles.author: Bridget Brown, Lorian Belanger, Vanessa Gera, Cara Anna, Luena Rodriguez-Feo Vileira, Drew Callister, Lisa Marie Pane
articles.description: As the Iran war persists, U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said a U.S. submarine had sunk an Iranian warship, the first such attack on an enemy since World War II.
articles.title: Live updates: Hegseth says US submarine sank Iranian warship with torpedo in international waters

articles.author: Suman Naishadham, Joseph Wilson
articles.description: Spain’s foreign minister has denied that the government has changed its position on supporting the U.S. military operations against Iran. Foreign Minister José Manuel Albares' comments on Wednesday contradicted a White House spokesperson. He told Spanish radio station Cadena Ser that "I can refute (the White House spokesperson.” Albares said that “the position of the Spanish government regarding the war in the Middle East, the bombing of Iran and the use of our bases has not changed one iota.” Albares spoke in Madrid shortly after White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt had said that Spain had changed its position and “they’ve agreed to cooperate with the U.S. military.”
articles.title: Spain denies cooperation with US operations in Mideast

articles.author: David Bauder, Meg Kinnard, Ali Swenson
articles.description: Some of the sharpest criticism President Donald Trump has faced in the early days of the Iran war has come from once-loyal media figures more accustomed to singing his praises. The conservative media infrastructure has grown so influential and valuable to the Republican president when everything runs smoothly that it's noticeable when there's discontent. Tucker Carlson, Megyn Kelly and Matt Walsh are among the notable figures to express some dismay, much of it centered around the influence of Israel. Walsh has also said the messaging around the war was muddled. Trump says he knows MAGA best and these objections don't reflect the views of his supporters.
articles.title: Iran war shows cracks in Trump's conservative media support

articles.author: Qassim Abdul-zahra Stella Martany, Rashid Yahya
articles.description: Kurdish Iranian dissident groups based in northern Iraq are preparing for a potential cross-border military operation in Iran, and the U.S. has asked Iraqi Kurds to support them, Kurdish officials have told The Associated Press. Kurdish party leaders have also discussed the Iran crisis with President Donald Trump, according to Iraqi Kurdish officials. One official says Trump has asked them to open the border and back the groups militarily. Iraqi Kurdish leaders fear Iranian retaliation. Recent drone and missile strikes have already hit the region, damaging homes, and disrupting electricity. Iraq also reinforces the border and the Iraqi government has vowed to stop infiltrations.
articles.title: Kurdish dissident groups say they are on standby to join fight against Iran

articles.author: Alanna Durkin Richer
articles.description: The House Oversight Committee has voted to subpoena Attorney General Pam Bondi to answer questions over the Justice Department’s handling of files related to the Jeffrey Epstein sex trafficking investigation. Five Republicans joined Democrats on Wednesday support the subpoena proposed by GOP Rep. Nancy Mace of South Carolina in a sign of continued frustration with the department’s review and release of a tranche of documents regarding the wealthy financier. The Justice Department had no immediate comment on the subpoena.
articles.title: House committee votes to subpoena Pam Bondi over Epstein files

articles.author: Bill Barrow, Fernanda Figueroa, Thomas Beaumont
articles.description: Texas Democrats have picked James Talarico as their Senate nominee, and the state lawmaker now becomes a national voice against President Donald Trump. Talarico won the primary Tuesday over Jasmine Crockett, and the congresswoman is urging Democrats to stay united. Talarico says his victory was driven by a push to change politics. He's emphasized unity, faith language and a “top versus bottom” message. Some voters did prefer Crockett’s sharper attacks on the Republican president. Talarico now faces a tough general election in a state where Democrats rarely win statewide. He will face either Sen. John Cornyn or state Attorney General Ken Paxton in November.
articles.title: Texan James Talarico becomes a fresh face of Democrats' midterm hopes

articles.author: Steven Sloan
articles.description: Primary elections set up major Senate fights in Texas and North Carolina, and they also expose fresh tension over voting rules. North Carolina locked in a high-stakes matchup as Democrat Roy Cooper faces Republican Michael Whatley. The race helps decide Senate control in November. In Texas, Sen. John Cornyn heads to a May 26 runoff against Ken Paxton. Big money keeps flooding the contest. Voting also turned chaotic in Dallas and Williamson counties. New precinct rules confused voters and triggered lawsuits.
articles.title: Election 2026: Takeaways from first votes of the campaign
